palette_hint: Deep navy #0a1628 background, crisp white #f0f4ff text, electric teal #00d4b8 and amber #f5a623 as accent colors
type_style: System sans-serif (Arial, Helvetica) for body at 16px/1.6, bold 700-weight geometric headings, clinical and precise feel
description: An interactive infographic site called 'Abyssal Atlas' presenting data-driven visual essays about Earth's ocean systems, using inline SVG diagrams, clipped depth cross-sections, and path-drawn marine silhouettes.
constraints: Max content width 1100px centered, with full-bleed #0a1628 navy background on every page
Every page must feature at least one inline SVG illustration occupying no less than 400px height with custom paths — no rectangles or circles alone
Navigation bar uses a clip-path: polygon(0 0, 100% 0, 100% 80%, 95% 100%, 0 100%) cut at the bottom edge, colored #0d2040, 60px tall
Section dividers use skewed CSS transforms (skewY(-3deg)) on colored bands — teal #00d4b8 at 40% opacity — to break the grid
All data callout numbers (e.g. '3,800m average depth') must appear inside SVG-drawn hexagonal badge shapes filled with #f5a623, white text

page_specs.overview: The landing page presents a full-width inline SVG world map (Mercator projection drawn with simplified continent path outlines in #1a3a5c) with ocean basins labeled and teal fill on the water areas. Three amber hexagon badges overlay the map showing '361 million km²', '97% of Earth's water', and '3,800m average depth'. Below the map, a horizontal strip of five icon-glyphs — each a custom SVG path representing a wave, a fish, a thermometer, a pressure gauge, and a DNA strand — introduces the five sections of the atlas.
page_specs.currents: A full-height inline SVG diagram depicts the global thermohaline conveyor belt as animated-looking curved arrow paths (using SVG stroke-dasharray for dashed flow lines) in teal and amber, sweeping across a simplified globe outline. A sidebar column lists six named currents (Gulf Stream, Humboldt, Kuroshio, Agulhas, Antarctic Circumpolar, Labrador) each with a small SVG path icon and a stat badge. The background uses an SVG radial gradient from #0d2040 to #0a1628 defined inside a <defs> block.
page_specs.depth-zones: A vertical cross-section SVG illustration (800px tall, full-width) shows five ocean depth zones — Sunlight (0–200m), Twilight (200–1000m), Midnight (1000–4000m), Abyssal (4000–6000m), Hadal (6000–11000m) — as horizontal bands with graduated fills defined via SVG linearGradient, darkening from teal-tinted #1a4a6e at the surface to near-black #020810 at the trench. Each zone band is clipped with a subtle wave-path edge at its bottom border. Silhouettes of representative creatures (whale, lanternfish, anglerfish, sea cucumber, amphipod) are drawn as SVG filled paths positioned at the correct depth.
page_specs.biodiversity: A radial SVG diagram resembling a phylogenetic wheel (600px × 600px, centered) branches outward from a central 'Marine Life' label into six taxonomic groups: Fish, Cephalopods, Cnidarians, Echinoderms, Marine Mammals, Microbes. Each branch terminates in a leaf-shaped SVG path filled with teal or amber alternately, containing the species-count stat (e.g. '34,000 fish species'). The page background features a repeating SVG pattern of tiny hexagons defined in <defs> and applied as a fill on a background rect, giving a subtle honeycomb texture at 8% opacity.
page_specs.threats: Four threat panels are arranged in a 2×2 grid, each panel using clip-path: polygon() to create a parallelogram shape rather than a rectangle — alternating left-leaning and right-leaning cuts. Each parallelogram contains an SVG icon (melting ice cap, factory pipe, trawl net, plastic bag — all custom paths), a bold stat in an amber hexagon badge, and two lines of explanatory text. A footer SVG illustration spans full width showing a coastline silhouette with a rising sea-level indicator line in red #e84040, annotated with '+3.6mm/year' in a hexagon badge.
